babyDevelopment: The baby is considered 'early term.' Most systems are ready for life outside the womb. The baby is practicing sucking and swallowing. The digestive system contains meconium, which will be the baby's first stool.
bodyChanges: You may experience increased vaginal discharge as your body prepares for labor. Your cervix may begin to 'efface' (thin out) and 'dilate' (open). Some women experience 'nesting'—a sudden burst of energy to clean and organize.
tips: Ensure you know the signs of early labor

do: Relax and manage stress through deep breathing
dont: Ignore vision changes or severe headaches

travelAdvisory.note: Stay very close to your birthing facility.

milestoneDescription: You've reached 'early term'—most babies born now do very well.
